entry.title: SAP CPI Production Order Creation Fails Due to Invalid Time Format in OData Response
entry.content:
Hi team,

I'm working on a SAP CPI iFlow that creates production orders in SAP S4H, using the Production Order (Version 2) API.

I started by testing the connection between CPI and SAP, so I only have a Content Modifier with the payload and a Request Reply to connect to the OData adapter.

The payload is as follows:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<A_ProductionOrder_2>
<A_ProductionOrder_2Type>
<ManufacturingOrderType>ZP01</ManufacturingOrderType>
<Material>SG002</Material>
<ProductionPlant>3516</ProductionPlant>
<MRPController>001</MRPController>
<MfgOrderPlannedStartDate>2024-09-15T00:00:00</MfgOrderPlannedStartDate>
<MfgOrderPlannedStartTime>00:00:00</MfgOrderPlannedStartTime>
<MfgOrderPlannedEndDate>2024-09-16T00:00:00</MfgOrderPlannedEndDate>
<MfgOrderPlannedEndTime>00:00:00</MfgOrderPlannedEndTime>
<ProductionUnit>PC</ProductionUnit>
<TotalQuantity>10</TotalQuantity>
</A_ProductionOrder_2Type>
</A_ProductionOrder_2>

With this payload, I'm successfully creating production orders, so it doesn't seem like the payload is incorrect. However, CPI is always returning the following error:

com.sap.gateway.core.ip.processor.exception.ODataProcessingException: com.sap.gateway.core.ip.processor.exception.ODataProcessingException: Exception occurred in converting [class java.util.GregorianCalendar] with value [java.util.GregorianCalendar[time=?,areFieldsSet=false,areAllFieldsSet=false,lenient=false,zone=sun.util.calendar.ZoneInfo[id="Etc/UTC",offset=0,dstSavings=0,useDaylight=false,transitions=0,lastRule=null],firstDayOfWeek=1,minimalDaysInFirstWeek=1,ERA=?,YEAR=?,MONTH=?,WEEK_OF_YEAR=?,WEEK_OF_MONTH=?,DAY_OF_MONTH=?,DAY_OF_YEAR=?,DAY_OF_WEEK=?,DAY_OF_WEEK_IN_MONTH=?,AM_PM=?,HOUR=?,HOUR_OF_DAY=24,MINUTE=0,SECOND=0,MILLISECOND=?,ZONE_OFFSET=?,DST_OFFSET=?]] to String., cause: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: HOUR_OF_DAY

I believe the issue is related to two tags in the Request Reply response: MfgOrderPlannedEndTime and MfgOrderScheduledEndTime, with the value PT24H00M00S. It seems that java.util.GregorianCalendar only accepts hours in the range of 0-23, but according to SAP documentation, the hour 24:00 is allowed (which seems incorrect to me).

I'm struggling to find a solution for this issue, as it seems to be a very basic error.

Thanks in advance for your help.

entry.title: No Authorization to Start the UI for Transacation
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

We are trying to access some configuration in CBC Example:

Error: No Authorization to Start the UI for Transaction


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: No Authorization to Start the UI for Transacation
entry.content:
No access to configuration activities
Assign Catalog for configuration activity for area concerned

 

Configuration activities For accounts payable configuration activities business catalog  SAP_CA_BC_IC_LND_FIN_AP0_PC - Accounts Payable - Configuration is needed 
Example configuration activities 
Set Up All Company Codes for Payment Transactions   S_ALR_87100688
Set Up Payment Methods for Each Country/Region for Payment Transactions  S_ALR_87100689
Set Up Payment Methods per Company Code for Payment Transactions S_ALR_87100690

For Cash Journal - System Specific configuration 
Cash Journal - System Specific Configuration SAP_CA_BC_IC_LND_FIN_CJ1_PC
Cash Journal - Configuration SAP_CA_BC_IC_LND_FIN_CJ0_PC is needed 
 
 Example configuration activity 
  Maintain Business Transactions  S_ALR_87100687
  
For Asset Accounting - Configuration SAP_CA_BC_IC_LND_FIN_AA0_PC is needed for 
example configuration activity  Define asset Class   

See also https://me.sap.com/processnavigator

SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Accelerators 
-> Implementation -> Configuration apps

entry.title: Re: How many user use Single SAP ID at same time SAP Business One

entry.title: crystal report footer blank space at end
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have added a report footer into an existing crystal report with terms.  when I print the report its prints 1st page with all header and details and 2nd page just report footer but its giving totally 1/3 in display but when i print its prints only 1 & 2nd page and the third one is just blank no page.  As anyone faced this issue please help me.  following is the screenshot of the report footer and output.

entry.title: What You Must Know About SAP Signavio Process Insights, Discovery Edition
entry.content:
Dear Community,

This blog provides an overarching overview on the key facts on what you must know about SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition!

Starting off with:


What is SAP Signavio Process Insights?
SAP Signavio Process Insights is a cloud solution running on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) that delivers data-driven insights into business processes and their usage based on data from one or more source systems. It helps organizations to achieve business process excellence by helping to identify where processes can be improved, allowing users to drill down to understand root causes, and providing recommendations on how to improve. It allows users to navigate through their business process transformation journey from insight to action to maximize process excellence.


What Is SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition?
The discovery edition of SAP Signavio Process Insights gives you a first look at the performance of your business processes as well as recommendations for improvements before you commit to the full solution. It allows you to start exploring a reduced set of process performance data using the SAP Signavio Process Insights application with minimal onboarding.

You extract a limited data set for a selected number of process performance indicators from your ERP system, validate the data, and send it to SAP.

Request your analysis here: https://url.sap/discovery-edition.

We recommend reading the Get-started blog because it provides step-by-step guidance on how to request the discovery edition. After five business days, you receive access to a dedicated discovery edition tenant that has been created for you and into which your data has already been imported. To expand the perspectives on the analysis, you can invite experts from within your organization.


How does the discovery edition differentiate itself from SAP Signavio Process Insights (full edition)?
Take a look at the overview to get an overall understanding of the main differences:

Want to read the full blog? Access it here: The difference between SAP Signavio Process Insigh... - SAP Community


SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition is the ideal starting point when you want to:

  • Analyze your Business Processes
  • Transit from your SAP ERP system to SAP S/4HANA
  • Optimize your SAP S/4HANA system

Your value from SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition analysis 

SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition offers a one-time free of charge* analysis with unique tailored insights into your current operational business process performance, based on data from your SAP ERP or SAP S/4HANA system. Hence, the analysis provides ways in which operational efficiency objectives can be achieved - a critical point for staying competitive in today’s dynamic markets - by outlining specific insights and recommendations segmented by line of business and end-to-end processes.

The value analysis outlines the estimated benefits (potential monetary gains) that can be realized through process optimizations and improvements.

More functionalities such as automation, intelligent technologies, SAP Fiori apps, and all the SAP cloud solutions are available with SAP S/4HANA, enabling enhanced operational efficiency and innovation.

To broaden your knowledge, we recommend reading these blogs:

Executive summary
Part I: Introductory blog designed for business decision makers to get a comprehensive understanding of the discovery edition: Receive and present a clear picture of your proces... - SAP Community

Part II: New topics within the Executive summary (AI recommendations, HR recommendations): NEW PDF Executive Summary now delivered by default... - SAP Community


Blog series of SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition
Consisting of a total of eleven blogs, the series will comprehensively explain all important aspects of SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition analysis, with the help of visuals: Introducing Blog Series of SAP Signavio Process In... - SAP Community

Start your transformation journey to SAP S/4HANA:
Part I: This blog is relevant for you want to prepare the transformation of your SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA system: Move to SAP S/4HANA with SAP Signavio Process Insi... - SAP Community

Part II: This blog is relevant for you when you have already an existing SAP S/4HANA system and aim for updating it to the latest version: Innovations for live SAP S/4HANA systems with SAP ... - SAP Community

Request your analysis: SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition

Please keep in mind that SAP partners can no longer request SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition on behalf of their customers for legal reasons.

Any questions?
Feel free to reach out by sending an email to: Signavio-Discovery@sap.com or leave a comment in the comment section.

Kind regards, 
SAP Signavio Discovery team

entry.title: How to display ALV GRID with only structure
Re: crystal report footer blank space at end
entry.content: I have added following formula at page header section PageNumber >= TotalPageCount -1 otherwise the terms will print along with header from 2nd page and extend to 3rd page

There is a demo storefront for the B2B composable storefront. 
https://composable-storefront-demo.eastus.cloudapp.azure.com:543/powertools-spa/en/USD/login/register

Nevertheless the registration is somehow broken or deactivated and test users from the sample data that I tested are not working.

Does anyone have information about how we can use the B2B demo storefront with valid logins and different roles?

entry.title: Problems with the Message Summary Alerts window
entry.content:
Hi

SAP Business One 10.0 version for SAP HANA (10.00.150) FP 2102 (64-bit)

A user minimizes the Messages and Alerts window and continues working in Sap.

After a few minutes it returns to the messages and alerts window (maximize).
this one appears with a white screen.

The problem only occurs for one user (30 users on the company)

Any suggestion ?

entry.title: How is the "Missing Hours" is being calculated in "Manage My Timehseet" application ?
entry.content:
Hi All,

We would really like to know in "Manage My Timesheet" app how is the Missing Hours which displays in the overview of the App Tile is calculated ? 

Also, is there any way we also can check where is this missing hours flowing from ?

We have done internal analysis on the reporting perspective and see that planned hours and actual hours are matching; but we are not really sure where is this missing hours coming from ?

Any lead on this topic is much appreciated.

Regards,

Evans Aarons

entry.title: Re: Balance carryforward of actual data from SAP to SAC P...
entry.content:
Hi Gavin,

IFIACTPLNJEIC is  the technical view name of CDS view I_ActualPlanJrnlEntryItemCube. Not sure how you consume this in SAC where the S/4HANA import connection is based on OData services. Probably you are using OData service API_JOURNALENTRYITEMBASIC_SRV that uses the above CDS view underneath. However, this OData service and the CDS view underneath are not suited for extracting the opening balance. Therefore we have a separate OData service UI_FINS_GLACCOUNTFLOW. See documentation https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/48f4b4785b8e45938ac44a67be8032d9/3020da2c5b1b48d88c3b5c3fb8ad43bc.html

Best regards
Hartmut

entry.title: Visibility work seniority result
entry.content:
In the last release (1H 2024) SAP released new functionality to calculate employee work seniority based on MDF objects (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_RELEASE_INFORMATION/8e0d540f96474717bbf18df51e54e522/3d58995c3ad540d5b18d24f112a7c577.html?parentHref=/whats-new/8fcf4960eea24f78b1d7613da406a885?Version=1H%202024&q=seniority&locale=en-US&parentName=SAP%20SuccessFactors%20What's%20New&locale=en-US).

This is great functionality to calculate the seniority; but it seems that there is no UI available to make the results visible to employees/managers on the people profile (no element to add and it does not seem possible to create a custom UI for the MDF object). Are we missing something? In the implementation guide I also don't see anything around visibility of the result (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_EMPLOYEE_CENTRAL/c18eb6c8febd4d7c924db8a257ce940d/37d55f65235c4f6690241f45f68055bb.html?q=seniority&locale=en-US).

entry.title: Showing incumbents who moved internally
entry.content:
Hi there,

In my story report I can pull in all active and terminated incumbents of a position. This is not a  problem.  
However, I am struggling to pull in an active incumbent who has moved internally to another position in the following scenario. 

Employee A is employed in the Position 1234 from 1 January 2024 to 30 June 2024. 
Employee A then vacates Position 1234 to move into a new role in Position 5678 on 1 July 2024 and remains employed currently. They were never terminated, just moved from one position to the other. So there is no termination record attached to Employee A. 

When I run a report for Position 1234, it returns that there are no prior incumbents ? Employee A does appear as an active incumbent for Position 5678. 

How do I ensure Employee A is captured as an incumbent for all previously held positions?

My data query structure is as follows: 
Position -> Incumbents (under Employment) -> Personal Information

Any help appreciated. 
Thanks 
Ais

entry.title: Re: Modernize your BW Landscape: SAP BW/4HANA and SAP Datasphere with SAP BW Bridge
entry.content: @MKreitlein Hi Martin, SAP Release and Maintenance Strategy specifies different maintenance strategies for each solution.

entry.title: Re: Modernize your BW Landscape: SAP BW/4HANA and SAP Datasphere with SAP BW Bridge
entry.content: @MinheeSung Semantic onboarding to me just means empty shell with nothing under. I.e. you will have to rebuild / re-engineer everything in the query layer of SAP BW. Also if you use SAC you will have to rebuild your stories as you can't exchange a datasource from BW with one from another source. All in all I see semantic onboarding as empty as the solution itself. It's like a concept of a plan.

entry.title: Re: Restricting Multiple value using Constraint
entry.content: Thank you very much for your responses.

entry.title: download CSV I get can't load plugin
entry.content:
when I try and download the file using the link below I get an error couldn't load plugin.  Any idea which plugin I need? 

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/data-warehouse-cloud-1-begin-trial/jcr:content.github-proxy.1715048737.file/SalesOrders.csv

entry.title: Re: ADT debug - Create breakpoint at FM or Class method
entry.content:
Correct, this is not supported in ADT.

The quickest way to achieve a similar result in ADT would be this:

  • Use Ctrl-Shift-A (on Windows, I'm not sure about the shortcut on Mac) and enter the class name / report name / etc. in order to open the source code in the editor
  • Then use Ctrl-O and enter/select the method name / form name / etc. in order to jump to the relevant code block
  • Then double click on the left side of a code line to create a normal line breakpoint

In general ADT shows other breakpoint types (besides normal line breakpoints) in the breakpoints view: 

Best regards,
Armin

entry.title: Re: Restricting Multiple value using Constraint
entry.content: I will try your method, in our test system

entry.title: Where can I start learning SAP Enterprise Architecture Framework e.g. how it is structured?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Applying the SAP Enterprise Architecture Framework learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey explains the SAP Enterprise Architecture Framework and how it is applied to accelerate architecture engagements. You will learn and apply the skills and methodology to define a company-specific roadmap while considering the business strategy and priorities.

Learning objectives

After completing this learning journey, you will be able to

  • Explain the SAP Enterprise Architecture Framework, how it is structured, and how to tailor it for architecture engagements
  • Create an architecture vision of capabilities and business values considering the SAP Reference Architecture
  • Define a target business architecture enabling to achieve the business goals
  • Build an SAP Solution Architecture based on the business architecture and architecture vision
  • Define a technology architecture and create an architecture roadmap

Prerequisites

  • TOGAF foundation knowledge, preferably TOGAF certified
  • Good understanding of business strategy, organization, functional and process concepts
  • Good understanding of IT strategy, organization, applications, data, and technology concepts

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Unable to Update manually added Standard Fields to St...
entry.content: Hello Sijin,

entry.title: local setup commece cloud 2211 build failure groovyc
entry.content:
facing below error while setting up sap commerce cloud 2211 locally windows

 

[groovyc] org.codehaus.groovy.control.MultipleCompilationErrorsException: startup failed:
  [groovyc] General error during conversion: class groovy.transform.AnnotationCollectorMode cannot be cast to class groovy.transform.AnnotationCollectorMode (groovy.transform.AnnotationCollectorMode is in unnamed module of loader groovy.lang.GroovyClassLoader @3a2b459a; groovy.transform.AnnotationCollectorMode is in unnamed module of loader org.apache.tools.ant.AntClassLoader @26c89563)
  [groovyc]
  [groovyc] java.lang.ClassCastException: class groovy.transform.AnnotationCollectorMode cannot be cast to class groovy.transform.AnnotationCollectorMode (groovy.transform.AnnotationCollectorMode is in unnamed module of loader groovy.lang.GroovyClassLoader @3a2b459a; groovy.transform.AnnotationCollectorMode is in unnamed module of loader org.apache.tools.ant.AntClassLoader @26c89563)
  [groovyc]     at org.codehaus.groovy.transform.ASTTransformationCollectorCodeVisitor.lambda$findCollectedAnnotations$1(ASTTransformationCollectorCodeVisitor.java:187)
  [groovyc]     at java.base/java.util.Optional.map(Optional.java:260)
  [groovyc]     at org.codehaus.groovy.transform.ASTTransformationCollectorCodeVisitor.findCollectedAnnotations(ASTTransformationCollectorCodeVisitor.java:187)
  [groovyc]     at org.codehaus.groovy.transform.ASTTransformationCollectorCodeVisitor.visitAnnotations(ASTTransformationCollectorCodeVisitor.java:93)

 

tried with playing around with below configurations but did not help

groovyc.fork=false

<extname>.groovyc.fork=false

build.parallel=false

not sure if its log path issue. current hybris installation directory path 

 

C:\dl\core-customize\hybris\bin\custom

entry.title: Need to restrict the user's authorization to enable external access of BEx Query
entry.content: In SAP BW BEx Query we have option to check if we want to enable external access of the BEx query at query designer level, but in our organization, we have two types of users, developers & another Super user. All the users excluding developers should not be able to enable external access, OLE DB, OData & Easy Query at Query designer level. Can anyone help me with right authorization object & relevant selections to do this.

BR, Slava

entry.content:
Hello All

Hope your are doing well !

We have received a requirement from the client. They need to schedule background jobs on the cloud server, similar to how they do in the on-premises system. Could you please let us know if we can schedule jobs for both standard and custom programs, and ensure the job runs every day?

Thank you 

JP

entry.title: Re: Performance Monitoring in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi Saimukesh,

You can use the mapping table 'SPACE_SCHEMAS_V_EXT' to map the schema name to a 'SPACE_ID'.
This mapping table can be found in the 'DWC_GLOBAL' schema. 

The 'SCHEMA_NAME' can be found in the table 'M_EXPENSIVE_STATEMENTS'

Regards,

Joran de Vries

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Landscape of the Future
entry.content: Nice one. Thank you. I feel the as well that DSP is the way to go. Even BW has 20+ years of development behind.

entry.title: Re: What is the process of Back ground job in HANA Public cloud and How we can schedule

Then, can I use Material Documents API in BAS?

It's possible to use?

Best Regards,

KhaingZin

entry.title: Continuous Improvements Continue with Your Feedback
entry.content:
Since multiple years SAP collects anonymous user feedback in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. To tell the anonymous user directly what we did is impossible, therefore this blog is shedding some light on what feedback we received, the actions taken from there and what we intend to do in the next months.

A short repetition on how we collect end-user feedback in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Since 2020 we allow all users to provide feedback to SAP by clicking the feedback button in the top right area of your SAP screen. Additionally we started to push surveys (in average once a months) to end-users. Either rather generic questions on satisfaction, usability and usefulness via time-controlled surveys to all users or application triggered questions to only users of specific applications. In the later case we can either collect the more generic feedback, e.g. to better understand how an improvement is appreciated, or we can ask very specific questions to better understand the users preferences. Additionally, we also had asked in the time-controlled survey questions on user enablement/help or in regards to automation and intelligence to better understand how our users do experience the applications and to prioritize or bring up new improvement ideas.

As a result, there are multiple, often small ideas, that users share with us and that we appreciate and implement or put on our development plan:

  • In the applications "Transfer Stock - in Plant" (F1061) and "Manage Stock" (F1062) we put the implementation of editable fields on the roadmap, as users explained us a specific situation, where the originally taken defaults forces them into unnecessary work, that can be avoided.
  • The application "Create Billing Documents" (F0798) is planned to be improved in multiple aspects. A standalone button should allow the creation of proforma invoices, give more flexibility to add filtering options and finding a more prominent place for the PDF preview button. The better placement of the PDF preview button is also put on the roadmap for "Manage Billing Documents" (F0797), but additional item specific filtering options are planned.
  • A very tangible example in "Reprocess Bank Statement Items" (F1520) to the specific question "is there any information you miss on the screen that would make the application more supportive for your task?" one user suggested the VAT number. We now plan to add the VAT number to the worklist with 2502. 

Why did I talk about these small examples? Because you should know that we care also about the small suggestions that make your life easier, we take them serious and we work diligently on improving our product!

During the time from September 2023 through March 2024 we asked automation related questions and received more than 300 detailed comments where either users had been happy or where they suggested application specific ideas for improvement. Allowing mass changes or directly using the right default values already well describes how the usage of apps becomes efficient. While there was not that one big and genius idea, there were many solid suggestions where development can get very concrete ideas on how to improve a specific application.

For the next six months we plan to occasionally ask questions on SAP Joule, the AI copilot. Our intention is to collect your feedback on where Joule could help you in your daily work, how intuitive the interaction is and how satisfied you are in general with the answers you receive. We encourage you to provide continuously your feedback to help SAP to continuously improve our product.

Additionally please also feel free to comment here on how you like SAP to ask the people working in our product about their opinion?  Would you see special occasions, ehere we should ask the user? Would you like to provide personal feedback, so we could contact you directly? We appreciate your comments!

Earlier blogs on this topic:

Your feedback helps to improve SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

With highest Appreciation! Your Experience Feedback helps us improve Your Products

An Uber for Engineering?

entry.title: Quality Gate Reporting with SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Quality Gates are, as the name suggests, "gates" or checkpoints that are planned and carried out at a specific time. The goal is to check the status and content of a delivery object, based on quality criteria determined in advance.

For reporting, SAP is currently working on an expanded option to make "Quality Gate Reporting" even more efficient and effective. So it will be interesting to see what will follow. We have already been able to place some of our requirements and wishes for SAP.

Source: SAP Lab Preview

Now let's look at the options that are already available and can be used in SAP Cloud ALM.
In SAP Cloud ALM, the Quality Gates are prominently listed on the project overview page with the associated progress status.

Source: SAP Cloud ALM S/4HANA Project

New Quality Gates can be created, edited and evaluated using the "Quality Gates" app.

Source: SAP Cloud ALM S/4HANA Project

All Quality Gates are listed in the list view and you can filter by them.

Source: SAP Cloud ALM S/4HANA Project

There is also the option of displaying the Quality Gates graphically on the timeline in a Gantt Chart.

Source: SAP Cloud ALM S/4HANA Project

In the detailed view of a Quality Gate you will find a "checklist" in which the relevant checkpoints are listed. These checkpoints must be checked and approved at the appropriate time.

Source: SAP Cloud ALM S/4HANA Project

I hope I was able to provide an insight and some suggestions on the topic of Quality Gate Management.

entry.title: Attempting to retrieve data using SQLQueries with more than one parameter returns no data
entry.content:
Working with the service layer and I have created an sql query to expect 2 parameters, as below:



While attempting to run the query via GET request, the response returns no value. However I have some data that should be retrieved.



(I created another query with hard coded values and it returns data as expected- below screenshot)

 

I am not sure what the issue is

entry.title: Re: What is the process of Back ground job in HANA Public...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for your question.

Here's general information: 

Job Scheduling: 
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/1161ad331e8f47478ba3537caf0af7f3/f08f4a507df34b228a98c45e25cca9b8.html?locale=en-US

More detailed information is available in Application Jobs:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/37e7a011a524405882af49cce79f0fb4.html?locale=en-US

There's a wide variety of job scheduling apps, for example, Schedule General Ledger Jobs:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/12107457ac955c08e10000000a441470.html?locale=en-US

Creating an Application Job describes how to create your own application job:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/2388da7c4aaf488ca1954f73fd8d40bc.html?locale=en-US

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Sales Order - Item Line FMS not working in CCC
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have created a Formula Manager System (FMS) in the SO_Item Line, where the data is supposed to be captured from the Item Master Data (UDF). The query works perfectly in another database, but when we tried it in a database that is in CCC, it did not function as expected. Both database is in Hana.

Here’s the query we are using:

 
SELECT "U_Contract" FROM OITM WHERE "ItemCode" = $[RDR1.ItemCode]

Could you please advise on why this might be happening and what adjustments might be needed for it to work in the CCC database?

Thank you for your help!

entry.title: Re: SAP Activate Project manager Certification
entry.content:
Hi @Jayesh65 

E_ACTAI - SAP Certified Specialist – Project Manager - SAP Activate for Agile Implementation Management 
Please access our SAP Certified Specialist - Project Manager - SAP Activate for Agile Implementation Management website for details 
This certification verifies that the candidate has the knowledge required in the area of SAP Activate agile project management. It validates SAP Cloud-focused advanced business skills and the ability to apply methodologies and tools. It also verifies that the project manager has skills to manage internal and external stakeholders, has basic knowledge of change management processes and can successfully manage the end-to-end transition of the project from Sales to Realization.
Please note: To prepare for this certification, it is necessary to take Learning Journey Discovering SAP Activate in addition to the Learning Journey(s) displayed under "how to prepare".

C_ ACT - SAP Certified Associate – Project Manager 
Please access our  SAP Certified Associate - Project Manager - SAP Activate website for details.
This certification verifies that you possess the skills required in the area of SAP project management. It validate project managers' business skills, communication, stakeholder management, change management, successful project transitions, and understanding of testing and quality management, especially for SAP-related projects, requiring the SAP Activate methodology.
Please note: To prepare for this certification, it is necessary to take Learning Journey Discovering SAP Activate - Agile Project Delivery in addition to the Learning Journey(s) displayed under "how to prepare".

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Folder sharing SAP B1 Cloud control center
entry.content: Thanks

entry.title: Re: Reorganizing of demand with T-code MD74 is not working

For example: Sales BOM Header from Plant A, and sub-items from Plant B & Plant C.

Piotr Retecki

entry.title: Introducing Data & Analytics Maturity Assessment of “SAP Data and Analytics Advisory Methodology"
entry.content:
In the last blog, I announced the new release of the “SAP Data and Analytics Advisory Methodology”. One of the improvements is the introduction of a Data and Analytics Maturity Assessment used in phase IV. This tool helps to evaluate data governance and organizational aspects that are essential for a successful realization of the value proposition defined for the business outcomes in phase II.
Thus, this blog provides guidance how to apply the maturity assessment to identify improvement actions that are considered in the roadmap afterwards.

Overview of the Data and Analytics Maturity Assessment

The “SAP Data and Analytics Advisory Methodology” focuses on data & analytics architecture development. But even the best architecture is ineffective when critical data governance processes, policies, key roles, and competencies are not established within the organization. Therefore, the methodology provides a general investigation of data governance-related topics to identify critical gaps that impact the architecture’s ability to deliver effective value.

This maturity assessment offers a total of 15 data governance and organizational focus topics from five dimensions, which can be assessed first from the current perspective and then for the desired future state. The assessment comprises five maturity levels, ranging from “initial” to “data-driven”.

Not all governance topics in context of the architectural investigation are equally important. It is sufficient to select and analyze the most relevant aspects.
The maturity assessment is used in phase IV of the methodology and is organized in two parts:

  • Part 1 covers topics related to critical data governance topics like data strategy, data quality & KPI’s, data architecture and processes.
  • Part 2 addresses typical data governance and management roles, their responsibilities, and interactions in an organization.

Let’s have a look into the two parts of the assessment in more detail.

Part 1: Assessing data governance focus topics

Data governance is a crucial framework that outlines the rules, processes, and accountabilities necessary for organizations to effectively manage their data. This comprehensive approach treats data as a product, ensuring that it meets specific standards and delivers value to both internal and external stakeholders. When data is handled in this manner, it is referred to as a "data product".
The key components of data governance are:

  • Rules: include standards, policies, and guidelines that set the foundation for data management.
  • Processes: the mechanisms that facilitate data management and decision-making on relevant topics.
  • Roles & Accountability: defines the responsibilities and decision rights for data management.

Managing "data as a product" involves overseeing its availability, usability, security, and integrity to deliver business value. The goal of data governance is to ensure the right data reaches the right person at the right time, and at the expected quality, enabling informed decision-making that drives business outcomes.

The first part of the maturity assessment starts to investigate the data strategy as a baseline to establish data & analytics as a core competency. This is followed by looking into KPI’s to track data strategy execution and assess data quality. Then data architecture is analyzed that is important to provide a common understanding of data. Finally, we investigate data governance processes to ensure sufficient data quality and deliver tailored reporting, analytics and business AI solutions.

Part 2: Assessing organizational focus topics 

Part two of this maturity assessment focuses on the dimension “organization”. Here you evaluate the readiness of the organization regarding roles and teams contributing to the data governance and identify improvement potential.

In a mature data-driven organization, data governance and management teams consist of several roles, each with distinct responsibilities and skills.
Examples include:

  • Chief Data Officer: responsible for maintaining data quality, security, and compliance, as well as promoting innovation and data-driven decision-making within the organization.
  • Data Steward: enforce data governance policies and procedures and ensure compliance with data regulations.
  • Data Domain Owner: manages a specific area or domain of data within an organization to ensure accuracy, integrity, security, and availability.

Such roles are part of the data governance, which is recognized as an overarching responsibility within the organization. Nevertheless, it is crucial to define the structure of governance boards and teams to establish and enforce data governance rules and policies effectively.
This structure includes:

  • Data Governance Council: provides strategic guidance of data governance programs, prioritizes data governance projects and initiatives, and approves organization-wide data policies and standards. Data Governance Council: provides
  • Data Governance Team(s): define Data Governance policies, roles, methods, processes, tools etc. (Framework), enforcing data standards and collaborating with operational teams to resolve governance issues
  • Data Domain Teams: apply data governance policies, rules, methods, tools etc. on daily basis, manage data product demand & requirements and ensure their data quality and security.

The "organization" dimension of the maturity assessment evaluates in how far such roles and teams are established in the company.

The following five focus topics are assessed:

Procedure of maturity assessment

Although the maturity assessment is divided into two parts the assessment can be executed in one session, e.g. in a workshop with relevant stakeholders. To perform the maturity assessment, the following steps are carried out:

  • Select data & analytics governance focus topics from the five dimensions relevant to the scope of the assessment and target architecture.
  • Assess current maturity for selected topics, either collectively or by the individual stakeholders.
  • Assess future maturity by discussing whether the current level of maturity is sufficient or whether a higher level is required.
  • If the current and required maturity levels diverge, then necessary actions must be agreed upon and incorporated into the final roadmap.
    The Data & Analytics Advisory Methodology provides a template with a detailed description of each focus topic and its maturity levels.

Here is one example where four relevant focus topics were assessed:

 

In case current and desired maturity levels are different you should document what the gap is and what actions need to be taken to get to the to-be maturity level.

In the example above a data strategy was not existing but was considered as essential to provide the guardrails and guidelines to improve overall data & analytics governance. Also, the skills and knowledge in the organization required to work with data and create business value (data literacy) was not sufficient. Thus, follow up actions were defined to organize a data strategy development initiative and define a training plan to improve data literacy.

If this general assessment leads to the conclusion that data governance needs to be investigated in more detail, please refer to related frameworks from SAP (SAP Data Management Framework) or SAP partners.

In the next blog I will explain phase IV of the “SAP Data and Analytics Advisory Methodology” in its entirety with a focus on developing the architecture roadmap.

entry.title: Re: FICO-Reset company code data before changing the chart of accounts
entry.content: Kindly refer KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/3464871

entry.title: Re: FICO-Reset company code data before changing the char...
entry.content: Kindly refer KBA 3464871 - Error "Reset company code data before changing the chart of accounts" is raised - SAP S/4HANA Cloud

entry.title: SuccessFactors Mentoring: Can administrators enrol mentors on their behalf?
entry.content:
Hello mentoring experts

I have a customer that wants to use supervised mentoring programmes and sign up the same mentors for each cycle. They will use a group to do this. What they then want to do is enrol those mentors into the programme on their behalf, so that the mentors do not have to go through the join up process each time?

Is there a way to do this please?

SAP SuccessFactors Succession & Development

entry.title: Re: Reclassification method does not consider Preparation...
entry.content: No - without this feature the reclassification filters out PCC data, i.e. behaves as if this new option would not be enabled.

entry.title: Currency conversion is not functioning incorrect setup issue with public cloud
entry.content:
Dear All,

I am writing to address an important issue we are facing regarding the currency conversions within our newly created company code in Hong Kong. As per the default settings in the SAP public cloud, the company code currency is set to HKD, while our intended currency is USD.

Following our previous discussions with SAP, we have attempted to maintain the functional currency as USD as guided by the relevant Snote. However, we are currently encountering discrepancies when transactions are posted in EUR; these amounts are being converted first to HKD and then to USD, which is resulting in differences that we cannot account for.

To resolve this, we need to ensure that the conversion is consistently made from EUR directly to USD without the intermediate HKD conversion. I would appreciate your insights or any further steps we can take to rectify this situation.

entry.title: Is it possible to integrate a sapui5 BarcodeScanner in CAPM application..?
entry.content:
Hi team 😃,

I was working fine with SAPUI5 framework based FIORI apps. Recently I have enrolled in a CAPM training. I got an doubt that Is it possible to integrate a sapui5 BarcodeScanner in CAPM application. I searched for proper technical answer but found nothing. I understood that when it is about data manipulation we can do awesome job with CDS based JavaScript ecosystem, but when it is about implementing custom applications like scanner, camera is it feasible through CAPM. I am sorry if i have posted without proper understanding, I am still learning.

SAPUI5

entry.title: Re: SAP Intercompany Settlement process in Condition contract in S4 Hana
entry.content: These tags would be better 'SAP Financial Supply Chain Management' 'FIN (Finance)'

entry.title: Re: Attachement API
entry.content:
Hi @Connie__ 

 

The API API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV does not currently support the Product Master for adding documents

Please submit a feature request if you would like it added

 

Best Regards

Enda

entry.title: I cannot preview layout (SAP B1 HANA)
entry.content: Some modules in SAP B1 cannot print preview layouts; for example, inventory transfer I preview by using system layout and crystal report, but the preview window does not display. Can you give some suggestions on how to check log or solve this problem? Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Calling UI5 transaction F0763A in web browser
entry.content:
This SSCUI opens in new pop-up window, it is most likely blocked by browser by default. Once pop-ups are allowed for the system address, SSCUI will open correctly.

In Edge/Chrome for example, there is an icon in the rightmost part of address bar, pop-ups can be allowed there.

Kindly refer KBA 3419948 - Getting information 'Calling UI5 transaction F0763A in web browser' when trying to access SSCUI 150002 Manage Chart of Accounts

entry.title: Re: Attachment size control
entry.content:
Hi @Connie__ 

 

Currently the size of files , what document type is not restrictable .

 

For allowed Mime Types , please check the post 

 

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/allowed-file-formats-for-dms-upload-in-s-4hana-public-cloud/qaq-p/12572077

 

Best Regards

Enda

entry.title: Re: Attachment size control
entry.content: adding tag SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Research and Development

entry.title: Re: Attachement API
entry.content: Adding tag SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Research and Development

entry.title: Fleet Management SAP PM - synchronisation with financial assets
entry.content:
Dear all,

I would like to automatically update fields between equipment master (Fleet managment) and financial asset master - especially license plate number.

Enhancements: AAPM0001 (transaction: SMOD), components: EXIT_SAPLAAPM_001 & EXIT_SAPLAAPM_002 were already used for updating other fields of equipment master (non-fleet mgmt.), that were not available in the standard list. 

Can this be also used for fields of fleet management? If not, what would be a valid alternative?

Thanks Sunnyi 🙂

entry.title: Dump Created while fetching data TSV_TNEW_PAGE_ALLOC_FAILED
entry.content:
Hi.

I am fetching data from below code.
While fetching from CDPOS ..Dump is created. 
As per my analysis ..What i understood is ..It is having memory issue. 
Table CDHDR is nearly having 27 Lakhs records. Due to this Dump is created.

My Question is without using Hana In Program itself is there any possible way to solve the issue. 
Some logic I am thinking.
1)First need to count total records from CDHDR.
2)Then while fetching CDHDR table i need to split, it into batches like up to 1 lakh records then remaining process like Fetching data from CDPOS and other remaining process need to complete, then remaining records again from CDHDR Need to process it.. until total records over.

But how to implement I would like to know?

SELECT objectclas,objectid, changenr, username, udate, utime, tcode FROM cdhdr INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_cdhdr)
WHERE objectclas IN @LR_objectclas AND username IN @s_user AND udate IN @LR_daterange.


IF lt_cdhdr IS NOT INITIAL.
SELECT objectclas, objectid, changenr, tabname, tabkey, fname, chngind, value_new, value_old FROM cdpos INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_cdpos)
FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @LT_cdhdr
WHERE changenr = @LT_cdhdr-changenr.

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5 PDFViewer Not Working : Underlying iframe was not found in DOM.
entry.content:
Just for information, I've just tried my application on EDGE and the PDF is displayed correctly...

So the problem is mainly on Chrome?

Any idea why ?

Dear Priyanka,

BR

entry.title: Analytics Cloud Top-N is not supported in a charts with structures
entry.content:
Hello 

I am having a live model connected to a query in BW4H, the query contains a huge structure for contribution margin calculation. On top of the model there is a story with a chart, the chart displays one measure and one entry of the structure. I would like to use a rank option but it is greyed out and says it is not supported. See attachment

Any workaround or idea?

entry.title: Where can I how learn the Drive service captures car mileage and what are the benefits?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Capturing Car Mileage with Drive on IOS (iPhone/iPad) learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey introduces you to how the Drive service captures car mileage and helps simplify the expense reporting process for efficient and accurate reimbursements.

Learning objectives

  • Explain what Drive is, its features, and its benefits for mileage capture.
  • Identify the recommended phone and tablet settings for optimal use.
  • Demonstrate proficiency in capturing mileage using the different options offered by Drive.
  • Identify and follow the steps to expense car trips created with Drive.

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

 

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: How to apply condition on subform without script language in adobe form in sap
entry.content:
Hello all,

I'v one adobe form, in which it has two output (design) based on the condition 1 and condition 2. I'm calling this form from ALV Report (driver code), i'm passing a value if that value matches to the condition 1 then "first output" should be displayed, and if that value matches to the condition 2 then "second output" should be displayed.
both the outputs are wrapped in subform and both are present on Master Page. and these subforms should get visible / hidden according to the condition.
Also in the "second output" it is compulsory to have QR code on LAST PAGE.
You can consider conditions like -
condition 1 = 'ABC'
condition 2 = 'PQR' 

 thanks in advance.

entry.title: CVI - DEBMAS/ CREMAS mapping (CL_MD_BP_MAINTAIN)
entry.content:
Hi gurus, 

is there a way to mapping fields from IDOC CREMAS/ DEBMAS into the structure of CL_MD_BP_MAINTAIN for CVI. 

I want to use method VALIDATE_SINGLE and then MAINTAIN as suggested from SAP guidelines, but i want to know if there's a way to map an IDOC CREMAS/ DEBMAS into the structures of this class automatically. 

Thanks!!!!!

entry.title: Re: SAP Adobe Forms - Question mark field
entry.content: Just in case someone has the same problem. I delete all the tables inside the form and recreate them with same names, bindings and other properties, and everything work fine.

entry.title: Re: What’s New in SAP Analytics Cloud Q3 2024

Hello Megan,

I hope you are well. I don't see any more articles on your blog about the new versions of SAP Analytics Cloud since the QRC Q3 2024. Are you no longer in charge of this topic? If so, can you tell me who took over the topic?

Have a nice day,
Jean-Louis

entry.title: occurs obsolete abap
entry.content:
hi,

I need to create a table that takes a structure that I created in SE11.
I used the Occors command:

DATA: gt_test LIKE ZTEST OCCURS 1000.

what can I use instead of occurs?

thank you

entry.title: Question regarding the migration to the CSD and third-party IDP
entry.content:
Hi Everyone-

 

We have a question regarding the migration to the CSD and third-party IDP.

In this blog it says that Customers would develop, design, develop, test and deploy automation or develop and execute manual procedures to update configurations needed on 3rd party IDP related to 3rd party cookie deprecation, and SuccessFactors SSO certification expiration.

Can you confirm that they still use their third-party provider in the future with the new CSD and that they don´t necessarily need to migrate to IAS? In other words, if they do the required updates in their third-party IDP, it is ok to not migrate to IAS?

 

Link to blog-  https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3459706

 

https://community.sap.com/t5/product-and-customer-updates/migration-to-sap-cloud-identity-authentication-ias-ips-innovation-alert-amp/ba-p/271407?emcs_t=S2h8ZW1haWx8dG9waWNfc3Vic2NyaXB0aW9ufE0wNUJXS0FRNVY4Q1BVfDEzODAzNzg5fFNVQlNDUklQVElPTlN8aEs

entry.title: Re: Error Printing a rpt in a Shipping
entry.content:
Appears to be a permission issue in their software.

I suggest contacting them for support.

entry.title: CAP: How to call remote service with same XSUAA in background job
entry.content:
I have two services A and B in different CAP apps that share the same XSUAA. I'd like service A to call the remote service B in a background job. This results in a 401 error as there is no auth token. 

  cds.spawn({}, async () => {
      const serviceB = await cds.connect.to("B"); 
      await serviceB.send("foo", {}); // 401 error
    });

Service B does have a destination configured

  "requires": {
    "B": {
      "kind": "odata",
      "model": "./srv/external/B",
      "[production]": {
        "credentials": {
          "path": "/api/",
          "destination": "b-api",
          "forwardAuthToken": true
        }
      },
  }

Remote calls to service B work as intended when initiated inside request handling, as there is an auth token to be forwarded.

serviceA.on("bar", async () => {
  const serviceB = await cds.connect.to("B");
  await serviceB.send("foo", {}); // works
});

Is there a way for CAP to handle this automatically or do I have to fetch the token manually and set it in the header?

entry.title: Re: Where can I find the change number used in MM12 trans...
entry.content:
Hello Allen,

 

You can goto MM02, and from environment menu click on display changes.

 

thanks,

entry.title: Re: Padding is invalid and cannot be removed
entry.content: And did you impliment the suggested work arounds on that post?

entry.title: Where can I start learning how to set up and use SAP Enable Now to create learning content?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Implementing and Using SAP Enable Now learning journey.

Overview
In this learning journey, you will learn how to set up and use SAP Enable Now to create sustainable learning content and provide performance support.

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, you can collaboratively perform the following in SAP Enable Now:

  • Implement and set up SAP Enable Now for producing learning content, both for strategic and project-based use. 
  • Create learning content in various formats such as simulations, quizzes, animations.
  • Edit learning content and on top of the formats listed above insert media such as videos or links to other resources.
  • Localize learning content.
  • Deliver sustainable learning material and performance support.

Prerequisites

  • As a prerequisite, familiarize with our SAP Enable Now discovery content on SAP Help Portal

 

Book your certification exam
Validate your skills and learning by completing the certification exam and gain a highly recognised accreditation.

 


Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Easy way to create APIs in SAP using standard classes
entry.content: Thank you for the good post. May I ask though, where is the API key, how does one get this? I can only see that you created a URL. I need to to generate an API key and am looking for blogs on how to do this.

entry.title: Re: in the Fressh Machine CRRuntime_64bit_13_0_22 fails t...
entry.content:
Refer to the supported Platforms page:

https://www.sap.com/documents/2016/06/f871031e-757c-0010-82c7-eda71af511fa.html

SQL Server 2019 is not supported until SP 27, you are using SP 22.

Upgrade your projects runtime, and no you can't just install SP 27, developer must rebuild the application.

entry.title: SAP Asset Manager - delta sync ignoring syclo changes sent by transport
entry.content:
I have noticed when

  • I change syclo configuration (/SYCLO/CONFIGPANEL ODATA -> Data Filter),
  • save changes to request,
  • transport request to Test system,
  • on mobile device perform delta synchronization, data are not synced correctly. New changes in the Data filter are not applied.

When I modify data on test system, delta synchronization will work. But If I do not change data but only changes in syclo were made, delta synchronization will not send data by current filter settings.

For example: Notifications => Item => Part group (list picker values) does not update values after delta sync. But if I reset the application and perform the initial full synchronization => list picker gets the correct current values.

I this correct behaviour of delta sync or am I missing something in syclo configuration?

entry.title: In SAC, using the Data Export API, how do I export the dimension hierarchies?
entry.content:
Hello everyone.

I have the Data Export working so I can export all the dimension members.   I'm using this endpoint:

/providers/{NamespaceID}/{ProviderID}/{DimensionName}Master | SAP Help Portal

I can get almost everything I need but I cannot see the hierarchies.  How do get those?  Is it from a different endpoint?  I looked through all the endpoints in the documentation it's not really obvious. I could try each in turn but I thought I'd ask before doing that.  

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Asking and Answering Questions in the Community After Migration
entry.content:
Hi, @Aruna9:

Please read the post on this page. You shouldn't be asking a question as a comment. You won't get an answer this way. 

We have a tutorial, FAQs, videos, and all kinds of resources to teach you how to ask questions properly in our community. Please take some time to familiarize yourself with the process and our rules (because you also posted the same question incorrectly here https://community.sap.com/t5/what-s-new/what-you-need-to-know-about-blogging-post-migration/bc-p/13863165/emcs_t/S2h8ZW1haWx8dG9waWNfc3Vic2NyaXB0aW9ufE0wWVRMMk5TODBFRE1BfDEzODYzMTY1fFNVQlNDUklQVElPTlN8aEs#M531, and that violates our rules of engagement).

Our members and I would like to assist you, but you have to participate in the community correctly in order for us to do so.

I ask that you please post the question in the proper Q&A board and report your two replies as inappropriate, so the moderation team can easily remove them from the site. 

Kind regards,

--Jerry

entry.title: Re: What You Need to Know About Blogging Post-Migration
entry.content:
Hello, @Aruna9:

Please see my reply at https://community.sap.com/t5/what-s-new/asking-and-answering-questions-in-the-community-after-migration/bc-p/13865116/highlight/true#M533 and follow the instructions that I provided there.

Kind regards,

--Jerry

entry.title: 🔵 Getting Started with Agents using SAP Generative AI Hub
entry.content:
"AI agents" have quickly become one of the most exciting topics in technology, sparking interest across industries.

In this session, we'll break down what AI agents really are, trying to bring some clarity to the concept while diving into their transformative potential. More than just theory, we'll walk you through practical steps for building and implementing agents using the SAP Generative AI Hub. Whether you're new to AI or looking for practical insights, this session will give you the tools to start using AI agents today.

Speaker: Mathis Boerner

entry.title: How to open the Fiori App "Create Purchase Requisitions" from a custom Fiori App and fill Positions?
entry.content:
Is it somehow possible to Open the Fiori App "Create Purchase Requisitions" from my custom Fiori App, which i created in SAP BAS and automatically fill some positions in the Purchase Requisition?

If it is possible please let me know how.

entry.title: Using SAP Cloud ALM for Service in SAP Cloud ALM, tenant extensions
entry.content:
As announced via this blog, SAP Cloud ALM, tenant extensions are now available for customers. This means that customers can procure as many tenants as they need.

For SAP Cloud ALM for Service, most content, including

  • which services will be delivered
  • what the results of these deliveries were
  • what the identified actionable items were

is sent into your tenant from SAP as explained in this blog. You as a customer have the option the decide for which of your customer numbers service information should be sent to which of your SAP Cloud ALM tenants (via tenant mapping).

SAP Cloud ALM, tenant extensions, are NOT available for tenant mapping. That means only standard SAP Cloud ALM tenants can receive service information from SAP.

In all SAP Cloud ALM, tenant extensions:

  • The "Service Delivery Center" will not contain any data.
  • The "Issues and Actions Management" app will not contain any data.

Customers using SAP Cloud ALM, tenant extensions, can only use their standard SAP Cloud ALM tenants for managing their service deliveries and working with the issues and actions identified during service deliveries.
